homepage.text_excerpt: --> Sarah Owen MP - Working for Luton North top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH Sarah Owen MP ​ Luton is my home and I love it. I am proud to champion our communities on the causes that matter. ​ bullhorn CAMPAIGNS From equality and human rights to jobs and opportunity, I am an active campaigner on ensuring Luton North residents get a fair deal. check-to-slot IN PARLIAMENT Alongside representing Luton North, I am also the Chair of the Women and Equalities Committee, fighting for equal rights for all. handshake HELPING RESIDENTS I help Luton North residents every day with issues including housing, benefits and policy queries. MY MISSION FOR LUTON NORTH circle-right High-quality jobs & opportunities for all Everyone deserves the best jobs and opportunities in their home town, no one should have to leave town to get the jobs they want. That's why I am always working hard to ensure we get the jobs, investment and training we deserve, for our young people, and for everyone else. circle-right Fighting for fairness & equality Luton North is proudly diverse, one town of many communities. I am proud to represent such a vibrant and generous town, coming together to mark significant times for all faiths and cultures. I know this togetherness can't be taken for granted - and will always fight to ensure everyone gets the dignity and respect they deserve. circle-right Making our streets safer I know crime is a major issue for many in Luton North. That's why I am working with our Police and Crime Commissioner and the Home Office to ensure Bedfordshire Police gets the resources it needs to fight crime and tackle antisocial behaviour. circle-right Building an NHS that is there when you need it I started my career as a care worker, so I know the importance of an NHS that works. From dentistry to urgent care, it is crucial that the NHS provides the services people in Luton North needs, and I am working with the government to ensure we benefit from the government's mission to fix the health service. WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH bluesky Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN © 2025 Office of Sarah Owen MP bottom of page

pages.text_excerpt: --> About | Sarah Owen MP top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. ABOUT ME I am the Labour Member of Parliament for Luton North, a constituency covering the neighbourhoods of Limbury, Lewsey, Leagrave, Sundon Park, Marsh Farm, Saints, Challney, Barnfield, Bushmead, Bramingham and Stopsley. ​ I was first elected in the General Election of 2019, and re-elected in 2024 to continue working hard as Luton North's MP. ​ I started my career working for the NHS, and it was working as a Healthcare Assistant and carer that gave me a strong appreciation for the dedication of our NHS staff. Throughout my career I’ve campaigned on getting a better deal for workers, on healthcare inequalities, human rights, antisocial behaviour, accessibility and road safety. I am proud to represent Luton North and champion our thriving and diverse communities. ​ In Parliament, I am also Chair of the Women and Equalities Committee , working to scrutinise the government in its policies that affect marginalised communities across the country. ​ ​ Luton is my home - I love it here and I am proud to represent this part of our diverse and vibrant town in Parliament. WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH bluesky Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN © 2025 Office of Sarah Owen MP bottom of page

pages.text_excerpt: --> Campaigns | Sarah Owen MP top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. CAMPAIGNS Both in Luton and in Parliament, I work hard to make change for the better. Whether that's through tackling anti-social behaviour, supporting schools and local businesses, leading inquiries on misogyny and racism, or changing the law to introduce bans on non-consensual intimate image abuse and time off for people who miscarry, I want to make people's lives better. Here is a selection of my recent campaigns. Fireworks Bill Antisocial fireworks are a plague on too many people's lives, both in Luton North and across the whole country. Our current licensing regime is overly permissive and impossible to enforce, with people being able to buy industrial-strength fireworks that can cause misery to pets, veterans, or anyone trying to get a good night's sleep. ​ My Fireworks Bill was introduced to Parliament in January, and aimed to restrict the noise level and potency of fireworks on public sale. Though the Bill did not have the time to progress to a vote, I am continuing to work with advocacy charities and campaign groups, alongside the government, to get this crucial change through. Speeding & Parking Antisocial driving is a big issue in Luton North, and I know the problems it causes for road safety and noise levels. ​ I have been working with the council and Bedfordshire Police, and petitioned for speed surveys, one of which was recently carried out on Quantock Rise in Bramingham. I will keep chasing to ensure that proper traffic calming measures are put in place on the roads that need it, and for better enforcement of parking violations, especially around school pick up and drop off. International Human Rights Luton is one of the most generous towns in the UK - and cares deeply about the plight of persecuted peoples in the UK and across the world. This is a concern I share. ​ From Palestine and Kashmir to the Democratic Republic of the Congo to Yemen and Syria, we live in an unstable world where innocent civilians often face untold suffering. In my role as co-Chair of Labour Friends of Palestine and the Middle East, I work on promoting human rights, and urging the government to take meaningful action through diplomacy to protect civilians. Miscarriage Bereavement Leave Long before I became an MP, I have campaigned for Miscarriage to be recognised as a bereavement in employment law, allowing women and their partners time off work to grieve and process. ​ Previously, those who miscarry had to take sick leave if they wanted time off, furthering negative stigma around miscarriage as an 'illness' and making women feel like there is something wrong with their bodies, as well as potentially leading to HR consequences. ​ I lead a Women and Equalities Committee inquiry into Miscarriage Bereavement leave, and am delighted that the government has accepted the principle and will introduce this crucial change as part of Labour's landmark improvement to worker's rights. Lifts for Leagrave Leagrave Station is used for hundreds of thousands of journeys every year as a major commuter hub. However, two of the platforms are inaccessible to anyone in a wheelchair, with heavy luggage, a pushchair or with any other accessibility needs. This is unacceptable. ​ I have been campaigning for lifts to be installed at Leagrave since I was first elected, and have made continued representations both to the previous and current Rail Ministers. We have made some progress, with a feasibility study complete and Network Rail actively exploring options, but progress has been too slow, and I will keep pushing to ensure we get the accessible station we need. Jobs & Growth Luton is perfectly placed to benefit from the government's new Oxford-Cambridge growth arc, with our proximity to London and transport connections making Luton an attractive investment. pages.h1: Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH
headings: h2: Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN
pages.text_excerpt: --> Contact | Sarah Owen MP top of page Sarah Owen MP WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH HOME ABOUT CAMPAIGNS CONTACT More Use tab to navigate through the menu items. CONTACT ME If you live in Luton North, I am here to help. Queries that I take up on behalf of local residents are called "casework". Ways I can help include: Housing, homelessness and tenancy support Benefits and other welfare payments Asylum, immigration and citizenship Local campaigns and support groups Education Health ​ When emailing, please include your full name and postal address . Parliamentary protocol dictates that I can only assist residents who live in Luton North. Click here to check if I am your MP. ​ My office treats everyone with respect and courtesy, and expect the same in return. You can read my office standards here . envelope sarah.owen.mp@parliament.uk We aim to respond to urgent casework as soon as possible. Please note there may be a delay in response to campaign or policy emails. phone 01582 488208 The office phone line is open from Monday to Friday, 10am to 5pm, excluding bank holidays. envelope-open-text 256 Marsh Road, Luton, LU3 2RX Please note that the office is not open to the public and visits are strictly by appointment only. ​ ​​ I know that many constituents may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to generate emails before sending them on to me. I know it can be a helpful tool. However there are limitations to be aware of that may make it more time efficient to email me directly, if you can. These limitations include: AI emails are generally longer, and thus take more time to process and respond to AI can be inaccurate in the steps it thinks an MP has taken or can take to help with or progress a case .AI may suggest an MP’s office take steps that would be unhelpful or counter-productive. AI may not fully be able to understand a response my office has sent and thus more correspondence (and more processing time) is required to respond to and progress cases. Use of AI What happens when I contact you for help? If you email my main Parliamentary email account, a member of my team will log your case with our caseworker software and make any inquiries on your behalf to assist with the matter as best we can. If you telephone our office, we will ask that you either email or fill out a written consent form for us to make representations on your behalf. We aim to reply to all emails and phone calls promptly. Please email media requests to my main Parliamentary inbox. For urgent queries please phone the office. Media enquiries WORKING FOR LUTON NORTH bluesky Promoted by Francis Steer on behalf of Sarah Owen MP at 3 Union Street, Luton, LU1 3AN © 2025 Office of Sarah Owen MP bottom of page
